detailed_analysis: These four files form an implicit adapter family (parse_X / render_X / extract_pair_id) for the four agentic-tool back-ends. None of them touch the filesystem — all parse/render is pure str->dict / dict->str. The audit focuses on (a) duplicated parsing/rendering logic, (b) divergent handling of the same logical conditions, (c) implicit-interface (Liskov) consistency, and (d) what the tests actually cover. The most serious structural smell is that claude_io is treated as the de-facto YAML-frontmatter library by the three other markdown-based adapters: opencode_io imports five private (_-prefixed) names from it, antigravity_io imports the same five, and codex_io.parse_codex_skill_md does a function-local import of FRONTMATTER_RE and _yaml_load from it. The second structural smell is a Liskov violation in the parse_X family. claude_io.parse_claude_md, antigravity_io.parse_antigravity_skill_md, and codex_io.parse_codex_{agent_toml,skill_md} all derive 'name' from the document content. opencode_io.parse_opencode_agent_md, by contrast, derives 'name' from the filename stem of an artifact_path parameter, and silently writes name='' when the path is omitted and there is no prior canonical. A caller treating all four parse_X as interchangeable will get an empty name from opencode. The third structural smell is divergent error handling for the same logical condition. The four adapters express the 'malformed root' contract three different ways. Encoding handling is divergent in a subtler way. claude_io._strip_bom_prefix handles both the real UTF-8 BOM and a 'corrupted BOM rendered as text' sequence. codex_io._normalize_toml_text duplicates the same two checks. Duplication of the parse-frontmatter prelude is real: the 7-line sequence appears verbatim in claude_io, antigravity_io, opencode_io._split_frontmatter, and codex_io.parse_codex_skill_md. opencode_io has refactored its own copy into a helper; the other three have not, and none of them share. The codex side is the structural outlier: it parses TOML rather than YAML-frontmatter-markdown, and it implements a hand-rolled TOML emitter. Test coverage realism: All three test files are synthetic-fixture-only — no real artifact off disk that came from the actual tool. tests/ contains no fixtures/ directory at all.

findings.variant: Liskov violation in implicit adapter interface
findings.problem: Only this one of four derives 'name' from a filesystem path. A caller that treats the family as an LSP-style interchangeable interface gets name='' silently when it forgets the keyword-only artifact_path. The contract is implicit; no Protocol/ABC declares it.

opencode_io imports five underscore-prefixed names from claude_io. antigravity_io does the same. codex_io does it at function scope. These five symbols are not Claude-specific — they are a generic YAML-frontmatter parser. The correct shape is a markdown_yaml_metadata_block module exporting these helpers as public API.

findings.variant: Shotgun Surgery — duplicated YAML-frontmatter parse/render prelude
findings.problem: The same 14-line parse prelude exists in four places. opencode_io has refactored its copy into _split_frontmatter; the other three have not. The four copies have diverged only in the error message. Same problem on the render side (3 copies).
The constant _CORRUPTED_UTF8_BOM and its handler are copy-pasted across two modules. A third BOM-corruption case will be fixed in one place and missed in the other.
tomllib.loads always returns a dict on success and raises tomllib.TOMLDecodeError on failure. The isinstance check is unreachable.

findings.variant: Divergent exception types/messages for the same logical condition
findings.problem: A consumer that wants to catch 'malformed input on any adapter' has no single exception type to catch. Four of the five paths raise ValueError; the codex-agent path actually raises tomllib.TOMLDecodeError because the ValueError branch is dead. The error-message strings are all different.

findings.variant: Hand-rolled TOML writer without a documented supported subset
findings.problem: Supports only str/bool/int/float/flat-list and silently rejects (TypeError) datetime, NaN, Infinity, nested lists, byte strings, None. The supported subset is not documented and not pinned by tests.

findings.variant: Silent fallback to empty name on missing artifact_path
findings.problem: When artifact_path is omitted AND prior canonical has no name AND the frontmatter has no name, the parser silently writes name=''. opencode docs say the agent's stable identity is the filename stem, so an empty name is meaningless.

findings.variant: Function-scope import to hide a dependency
findings.problem: Both parse_codex_agent_toml and parse_codex_skill_md import from agents_sync.canonical and agents_sync.claude_io inside the function body. No circular import exists. The lazy imports are masking the module's true dependency surface.

findings.variant: Primitive Obsession — implicit cross-adapter field schema as four frozenset()s
findings.problem: FOREIGN_AGENT_FIELDS / FOREIGN_SKILL_FIELDS hardcode the set of 'fields that belong to claude_io / codex_io and must not leak into opencode'. If claude_io grows a new field, this frozenset will not be updated automatically.

findings.variant: Round-trip coverage is render->parse only; no parse->render->parse and no field-order permutation
findings.problem: Round-trip stability is tested in one direction (canonical -> render -> parse -> canonical') but never in the other. No permutation test exercises this.

findings.variant: Synthetic-only fixtures — no real on-disk artefacts from the actual tools
findings.problem: All three unit-test files use inline string fixtures only — the parser is never exercised on a real .md / .toml that was actually emitted by the target tool.

findings.variant: Missing test coverage for artifact_path=None branch
findings.problem: The Liskov-outlier branch (CQ-01) is exactly the branch that has no test coverage.
16-key inline config literal duplicates whatever the syncer fixture builds elsewhere.

findings.variant: Magic-string field allow-list duplicated across modules
findings.problem: The field-name vocabulary is scattered across four modules as raw string literals. Note also the camelCase / snake_case mismatch: 'permissionMode' on Claude, 'permission_mode' on canonical, 'permission' on opencode; 'mcpServers' on Claude, 'mcp_servers' on Codex.

findings.variant: Magic string for legacy migration with no test for the marker-absent case
findings.problem: A user who happens to write the exact 75-char string inside a v0.4+ body will have that text and everything after it silently truncated on the next round-trip.

verdict_summary: No critical defects. Two structural smells dominate: (1) claude_io is the de-facto YAML-frontmatter library — three sibling adapters import five of its private (_-prefixed) helpers; (2) the parse/render frontmatter prelude is duplicated four ways, and a Liskov-outlier signature on parse_opencode_agent_md silently writes name='' when its keyword-only artifact_path is omitted — exactly the branch with no test coverage.
